articles.title: Is there still room for conservative treatment of Distal Biceps Tendon ruptures? What patients (and surgeons) should know. A case series with a mean 4-years follow-up.
articles.authors: Coliva F, Marinelli A, Riva M, Valentina V, Tortorella F, Minerba M, et al.
articles.population: Patients with distal biceps tendon ruptures treated conservatively (non-surgically)
articles.outcomes: Strength deficits (supination and flexion), pain during repetitive activities, DASH and MEPS scores, fatigue measured by dynamometry
articles.take_home: Conservative treatment for distal biceps tendon ruptures works reasonably well in sedentary patients with only minor strength loss, but patients doing heavy or repetitive manual work had worse outcomes and more pain, so surgery may be better for them. About one in five conservatively treated patients reported significant pain with repetitive activities.

articles.title: Propensity Matched Professional Baseball Pitchers With Early Trunk Rotation Demonstrate Increased Shoulder and Elbow Distractive Forces with Equivalent Ball Velocity.
articles.authors: Manzi J, Dillon M, Dowling B, Eskew J, Patel S, Kunze K, et al.
articles.sample_size: 339 pitchers (33 with early trunk rotation matched to 132 with late trunk rotation, creating 132 matched pairs)
articles.outcomes: Shoulder and elbow distractive forces, ball velocity, trunk and pelvis mechanics, prior injury prevalence
articles.take_home: Professional pitchers who start trunk rotation before their front foot lands experience greater stress on their shoulder and elbow than those who rotate later, but throw the ball at the same speed. This suggests their arms work harder to compensate for less efficient mechanics, and coaches should encourage pitchers to delay trunk rotation until after foot contact.

articles.title: All-Suture Anchors Exhibit Less Perianchor Fluid Formation Independent of Healing Quality Compared With PEEK Anchors in Arthroscopic Rotator Cuff Repair: A Propensity Score-Matched Comparative Study.
articles.authors: Gul O, Ayazoglu MA, Yazici A, Durusoy S, Gurun E, Okutan AE
articles.study_type: Retrospective comparative study with propensity score matching
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
articles.outcomes: Pain (VAS), function (ASES score), range of motion, tendon healing rates (Sugaya classification), and perianchor fluid collection on MRI at minimum 2-year follow-up
articles.take_home: Both all-suture and PEEK anchors produced similar clinical improvements and healing rates, but all-suture anchors had less fluid collection around the anchor site. Fluid collection around PEEK anchors was linked to poorer healing, whilst this link was not seen with all-suture anchors.

articles.title: Towards a common definition of frozen shoulder: a scoping review of randomized controlled trials.
articles.authors: Karasuyama M, Ishikawa H, Uchida T, Saka M, Muraki T, Minamikawa T, et al.
articles.study_type: Scoping review of randomized controlled trials
articles.population: Adults with frozen shoulder or adhesive capsulitis
articles.sample_size: 310 randomized controlled trials identified from 4,426 records
articles.outcomes: How frozen shoulder is defined across trials, including terminology used, age criteria, pain characteristics, range of motion limitations, symptom duration, and imaging requirements
articles.take_home: Frozen shoulder is defined very differently across research trials, making it hard to compare results between studies. Researchers need to agree on standard definitions covering age ranges, how much pain patients have, how much movement is lost, and how long symptoms have lasted.

articles.title: Temporal trends and estimated lifetime attributable radiation risk of preoperative planning computed tomography for primary shoulder arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Kalva SR, Fucich D, Perry AJ, Lezak B, Torkieh J, Joshi T, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary shoulder arthroplasty from 2016 to 2024
articles.outcomes: Annual percentage of patients receiving preoperative CT scans and estimated lifetime attributable cancer risk stratified by age and sex
articles.take_home: The use of preoperative CT scans for shoulder surgery has increased from 53% to 66% of patients over nine years. Whilst the cancer risk from these scans is small, younger women may have a slightly higher projected cancer risk than older patients.

articles.title: Shoulder Muscle Adiposity Can Predict the Efficacy of Corticosteroid Injections in Treating Adhesive Capsulitis After Rotator Cuff Repair Surgery.
articles.authors: Chen NH, Lin CL, Cheng YH, Chiu YS, Hsu TH, Huang SW
articles.population: Patients who developed adhesive capsulitis (shoulder stiffness) after rotator cuff repair surgery and received corticosteroid injections
articles.outcomes: Pain scores and range of motion at 8 weeks after corticosteroid injection; prediction of treatment failure based on fatty infiltration in shoulder muscles
articles.take_home: Patients with higher amounts of fat in their shoulder muscles before surgery are six times more likely to have corticosteroid injections fail to improve stiffness after rotator cuff repair. This finding may help doctors identify patients who need different treatments sooner.

articles.title: Is a 32 mm glenosphere optimal in reverse shoulder arthroplasty for patients with small glenoid anatomy? : a range of motion simulation study in petite Asian females.
articles.authors: Kiyomatsu H, Fujibuchi T, Kutsuna T, Kinoshita T, Tsuda T, Hayashi M, et al.
articles.study_type: Computer simulation study using 3D CT planning
articles.population: Asian female patients with small glenoid anatomy undergoing reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Impingement-free range of motion in extension-flexion, abduction-adduction, and external-internal rotation with different glenosphere sizes (32 mm, 36 mm, 39 mm) and inferior overhang positions
articles.take_home: In patients with small shoulder sockets, using a smaller 32 mm glenosphere with adequate positioning gave better predicted movement without bone-to-bone contact than larger sizes. This suggests smaller implants might work better for patients with smaller anatomy, but real-world testing is still needed.

articles.title: Does robust greater tuberosity repair affect the outcomes of reverse shoulder replacement in proximal humerus fractures? - A Bayesian network meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Ponugoti N, Selim A, Elhassan A, Jeavons R
articles.population: Older adults with proximal humerus fractures treated with reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Constant score, shoulder abduction, external rotation, forward flexion
articles.take_home: Repairing the greater tuberosity during reverse shoulder replacement improves shoulder function after fracture, but only if the repair heals successfully. If the repair does not heal, the results are similar to not attempting repair at all.

articles.title: The meta-analysis and systematic review of arthroscopic lower trapezius tendon transfer for massive irreparable rotator cuff tears.
articles.authors: Baek CH, Kim JG, Kim BT, Lim C
articles.population: Patients with massive irreparable rotator cuff tears
articles.outcomes: External rotation improved by 28 degrees on average, forward flexion by 37 degrees, pain reduced by 4.2 points on pain scale, functional scores improved, graft healing rate 89.4%, complications occurred in 3.7% of cases
articles.take_home: Arthroscopic lower trapezius tendon transfer improved shoulder movement, reduced pain, and had good healing rates in patients with massive irreparable rotator cuff tears. Complication rates were low and only 2.08% of patients needed conversion to reverse shoulder replacement.
